message:
feat(abi): add posix_close (POSIX 2024 §close explicit-error variant) (bd-zuy8)

POSIX 2024 added int posix_close(int fd, int flag) — an explicit-
error close that translates EINPROGRESS to success (per the spec
the fd is closed even when an in-progress operation reports that
errno, so EINPROGRESS reporting is purely advisory and most callers
should ignore it). Currently missing from frankenlibc-abi.

Implementation: 14-line wrapper over close() that:
  - Rejects non-zero flag with EINVAL (POSIX 2024 reserves all
    flag values; none defined yet).
  - Calls close(fd) then maps EINPROGRESS errno → return 0.
  - All other errno values pass through unchanged.

3 integration tests in unistd_abi_test.rs:
  - posix_close_zero_flag_closes_valid_fd: open + posix_close
    /dev/null cycle.
  - posix_close_unknown_flag_returns_einval: posix_close(-1, 1)
    returns -1 with errno=EINVAL without touching the fd.
  - posix_close_invalid_fd_returns_minus_one: posix_close(-1, 0)
    returns -1 with errno=EBADF (verifies non-EINPROGRESS errno
    passes through).

Verified via rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc_bd-zuy8):
  - 3/3 posix_close integration tests pass
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass
  - cargo clippy -p abi -D warnings clean
  - cargo fmt --check clean

Closes bd-zuy8.

@@ -774,6 +774,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-gwqja","title":"[MODE-4][MEDIUM] resource bounds: host thread trampoline spins unbounded","description":"[MODE-4][MEDIUM] resource bounds: host thread start trampoline spins without a bound while waiting for handoff

The host-thread bootstrap path contains an unbounded busy-wait:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1390-1399` loops on `start_ctx.host_thread.load(Ordering::Acquire)` until the parent stores a non-zero thread id.
- the parent only performs that store after `host_create` returns and after writing the thread id back to the caller (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1231-1252` and `1356-1380`).

Under normal scheduling this window is short, but there is no bound, no backoff, and no fallback park. If the creator thread is delayed under load, preempted in a bad spot, or stuck before the store, the child burns CPU in a pure spin loop.

That is a resource-bounds risk rather than a functional mismatch: it can turn thread creation pressure into avoidable CPU amplification.

Fix direction:
- replace the open-ended spin with a bounded handoff protocol, or
- initialize the host thread identity in a way that does not require the child to busy-wait on parent-side publication.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:46:28.884118055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:42:20.278149696Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:42:20.276052826Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["modes","pthread","resource-bounds","review"]}

Tighten the ABI regression from a broad errno family to an exact host-parity assertion for the null-path fast path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:37:35.038153706Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:39:27.706311440Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:39:27.702833586Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity mount_setattr(NULL) behavior: exact EINVAL instead of a broad errno family; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-h3pp8","title":"PORT: posix_close (POSIX 2024 explicit-error close)","description":"POSIX 2024 §close added int posix_close(int fd, int flag) — explicit-error close that returns 0 (not -1+errno=EINPROGRESS) when an in-progress operation prevents close. Currently missing from frankenlibc-abi. Implement as thin wrapper over close() that rejects unknown flags (POSIX defines none yet) and translates EINPROGRESS to success per the spec. Reference: glibc 2.38+ implementation in posix/posix_close.c.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T13:48:04.819591542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T13:48:04.819591542Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Zero divergences.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T07:00:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T07:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T07:00:00Z","close_reason":"Shipped in commits 12cbadb0 / 6c5c98ac.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential"]}
 {"id":"bd-h5x","title":"EPIC: Remove glibc callthrough (raw syscalls + pthread primitives)","description":"Critique mapping: #2.

Goal: eliminate dependency on system glibc for core I/O/threading primitives when we claim replacement.

Deliverables:
- Raw syscall layer for Linux x86_64 (no libc::syscall).
- Futex-based pthread mutex primitives.
- Migrate ABIs to use the raw layer.

Acceptance:
- rg finds no libc pthread mutex call-through in the ‘replacement’ build profile.
- E2E smoke passes.

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"RusticWolf session 27 (2026-03-09): Nativized 3 GCT symbols (175 remaining, down from 178):
- __call_tls_dtors: Native TLS destructor invocation via owned TLS_ATEXIT_LIST in startup_abi.rs. Drains in LIFO order matching glibc semantics.
- __idna_to_dns_encoding: Native RFC 3492 Punycode encoding for internationalized hostnames. Converts non-ASCII labels to xn-- ACE form.
- __idna_from_dns_encoding: Native Punycode decoding. Converts xn-- prefixed labels back to Unicode.
Added pub(crate) invoke_tls_dtors() in startup_abi.rs. Fixed pre-existing clippy issue in pthread_abi_test.rs (unnecessary unsafe block).
16 new integration tests: 12 IDNA (ASCII passthrough, Unicode encoding, Chinese, Japanese, roundtrip, null handling, case-insensitive prefix) + 4 TLS destructor (noop, invocation count, LIFO order, drain completeness).
All 154 glibc_internal_abi tests pass, 0 failures. 1915 total frankenlibc-abi tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"BlueLake","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:36:06.850671993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T03:36:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T03:36:00Z","close_reason":"All four children closed: bd-h5x.1 (call-through census), bd-h5x.2 (pthread primitive closure), bd-h5x.3 (non-threading call-through elimination + CI gate), bd-h5x.4 (errno routing). Epic deliverables (raw syscall layer, futex pthread primitives, ABI migration) are shipped and verified; this is the housekeeping closure of the parent epic.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","pthread","syscall"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-144","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-24ug","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-25n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-27kh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-33zg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-7ef9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-c1x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-cj0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-ef2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-yos","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x","depends_on_id":"bd-z84","typ uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:07Z"},{"id":1094,"issue_id":"bd-h5x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Heads-up from bd-yos lane: fixed missing ABI symbol exports (mlock2, name_to_handle_at, open_by_handle_at) that were causing release link failures under libc.map and blocking pthread stress gate runs. Also updated mixed detach/join stress expectation to current passing behavior (expected_exit now 0), and check_bd1f35_thread_stress.sh now passes 8/8. This should reduce false negatives for pthread callthrough-removal verification.","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:32:12Z"},{"id":1095,"issue_id":"bd-h5x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RusticWolf progress: converted strptime from GlibcCallThrough to native Implemented. Full POSIX format string parser supporting %Y/%m/%d/%H/%M/%S/%b/%B/%a/%A/%I/%p/%j/%C/%y/%e/%n/%t/%%/%D/%T/%R/%F. 8 integration tests pass (crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/time_abi_test.rs). GlibcCallThrough count reduced from 93 to 92 for time_abi family.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:28:11Z"},{"id":1096,"issue_id":"bd-h5x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RusticWolf progress: converted fnmatch from GlibcCallThrough to native Implemented. Full POSIX pattern matching with *, ?, [...] bracket expressions (ranges, negation), escape handling, FNM_PATHNAME, FNM_PERIOD, FNM_CASEFOLD support. 10 integration tests pass. GlibcCallThrough reduced to 101.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:35:20Z"},{"id":1239,"issue_id":"bd-h5x","author":"SnowyRabbit","text":"SnowyRabbit progress: converted six remaining stdio-vtable dlsym_passthrough symbols in glibc_internal_abi to deterministic native ENOSYS stubs (__overflow, __uflow, __underflow, __woverflow, __wuflow, __wunderflow), added focused ABI integration tests, and reclassified those six symbols in support_matrix from GlibcCallThrough -> Implemented. Verification (rch-offloaded): cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS; cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test PASS (71 passed); cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets -D warnings PASS. Workspace clippy currently fails on unrelated pre-existing dead-code in frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs (cached_check_orderings). cargo fmt --check also fails on unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in runtime_policy.rs.","created_at":"2026-02-27T04:26:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-h5x.1","title":"Call-through census + per-level forbidden policy map","description":"Background:
- Claiming replacement requires a precise accounting of remaining glibc call-through paths.

Scope:
- Generate complete call-through census by symbol/family/profile (interpose vs replacement-oriented builds).
- Define forbidden call-through list for each replacement level with explicit temporary exceptions.

Deliverables:
1) Call-through census generator and artifact format.
2) Per-level forbidden/allowed policy list.
3) Gap report mapped to implementation owners.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Census is reproducible and covers all exported ABI entrypoints.
- Every remaining call-through is classified as permitted temporary exception or blocker.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for census parser and classification logic.
- E2E run on smoke corpus verifying census stability.
- Logs: trace_id, symbol, family, callthrough_target, profile, policy_class.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:26.662924825Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T07:25:30.143863540Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T07:25:30.143841078Z","close_reason":"Acceptance met: call-through census/policy map aligned with support matrix + fixture coverage, and replacement guard/census gates now pass deterministically","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pthread","replacement","syscall"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.1","depends_on_id":"bd-7ef9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.1","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1097,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.1","author":"Codex","text":"Claiming bd-h5x.1 now. Initial gate status: scripts/check_callthrough_census.sh fails because tests/conformance/callthrough_census.v1.json is stale versus support_matrix. Working lane: regenerate census artifact deterministically, re-run guard, and record policy-map consistency evidence before closure.","created_at":"2026-02-22T07:14:15Z"},{"id":1098,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed policy/census alignment and removed non-allowlisted direct libc call-throughs. Evidence: bash scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh PASS (0 failures, 48 call-throughs confined to allowlisted modules); Check 2b PASS with 11 tracked families + 22 fixtures; bash scripts/check_callthrough_census.sh PASS (symbols=93 modules=11 waves=11). Code updates in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/{setjmp_abi.rs,wchar_abi.rs,dirent_abi.rs,signal_abi.rs,startup_abi.rs,dlfcn_abi.rs,stdlib_abi.rs,time_abi.rs,stdio_abi.rs}, scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh, tests/conformance/replacement_profile.json, tests/conformance/replacement_zero_unapproved_fixtures.v1.json.","created_at":"2026-02-22T07:25:29Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-nqoa","title":"Metamorphic proptests for pthread condvar contract state machine","description":"cond.rs currently has unit tests covering individual contract transitions but no property-based metamorphic tests. Add MRs analogous to bd-73gu's mutex coverage: (1) Signal/Broadcast never block, (2) Wait/TimedWait always block, (3) Destroyed absorbing state, (4) Uninitialized absorbing state, (5) Broadcast drains Waiting→Idle regardless of has_waiters, (6) blocks⇒errno==0 structural invariant. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/cond.rs #[cfg(test)] module. Parity with mutex contract MR suite (bd-73gu).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id": "bd-nsu-epic", "title": "PORT: ns_name_unpack + ns_name_pack DNS message codec to core::resolv::dns_name", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-04-25T10:35:32.608730Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T10:35:32.608730Z", "description": "Lift ns_name_unpack (decompresses RFC 1035 wire-format name following compression pointers) + ns_name_pack (writes uncompressed wire labels — no compression dictionary lookup in the current abi impl) plus the __ns_name_* glibc-internal duplicates from glibc_internal_abi.rs into the existing core::resolv::dns_name module. Total ~265 lines across 4 entry points."}
-{"id": "bd-nsu-1", "title": "PORT: ns_name_unpack sub-bead 1 — extend core::resolv::dns_name", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "epic": "bd-nsu-epic", "created_at": "2026-04-25T10:35:32.608730Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T10:35:32.608730Z", "description": "Add to core::resolv::dns_name: pub const NS_CMPRSFLGS: u8 = 0xC0; pub fn name_unpack(msg: &[u8], src_offset: usize, dst: &mut [u8]) -> Result<usize, NameError> following compression pointers from src within the bounded msg, returning bytes consumed (= src_offset advance, NOT the dst length). Pointer-loop limit of 128 jumps prevents infinite loops on malformed input. pub fn name_pack(src: &[u8], dst: &mut [u8]) -> Result<usize, NameError> walks uncompressed length-prefixed labels and copies them verbatim to dst (current abi does no compression). Tests: simple uncompressed unpack, compression pointer follow, deep pointer chain, infinite-loop rejection, src outside msg, label past eom, root-only, full round-trip pack(unpack(...))."}
-{"id": "bd-nsu-2", "title": "PORT: ns_name_unpack sub-bead 2 — rewire 4 abi shims", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "epic": "bd-nsu-epic", "created_at": "2026-04-25T10:35:32.608730Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T10:35:32.608730Z", "description": "Replace the inline ns_name_unpack, __ns_name_unpack, ns_name_pack, __ns_name_pack bodies (~265 lines) with thin shims that materialize msg/eom/src/dst as byte slices and call core::resolv::dns_name::name_unpack / name_pack. ns_name_compress and ns_name_uncompress (already simple compositions) need no changes — they still call the now-shimmed entry points. Verify ns_name_unpack* integration tests still pass."}
-{"id": "bd-nsu-1", "title": "PORT: ns_name_unpack sub-bead 1 — extend core::resolv::dns_name", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "epic": "bd-nsu-epic", "created_at": "2026-04-25T10:41:35.458643Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T10:41:35.458643Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-25T10:41:35.458643Z", "resolution": "Extended core::resolv::dns_name with: pub const NS_CMPRSFLGS = 0xC0; pub const NS_MAX_JUMPS = 128; pub fn name_unpack(msg: &[u8], src_offset: usize, dst: &mut [u8]) -> Result<usize, NameError> following RFC 1035 compression pointers from src_offset within the bounded msg slice, returning bytes consumed from src; pub fn name_pack(src: &[u8], dst: &mut [u8]) -> Result<usize, NameError> walking length-prefixed labels and copying verbatim. The MAX_JUMPS bound defends against malicious circular pointer chains. 14 unit tests cover simple uncompressed unpack, root-only, compression-pointer follow, circular-pointer rejection, pointer-past-eom rejection, label-past-eom rejection, src-outside-msg rejection, pack identity, pack rejection of compression pointer in source, pack label-over-63 rejection, pack truncated-src rejection, plus a unpack(pack(msg)) round-trip property test."}
-{"id": "bd-nsu-2", "title": "PORT: ns_name_unpack sub-bead 2 — rewire 4 abi shims", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "epic": "bd-nsu-epic", "created_at": "2026-04-25T10:41:35.458643Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T10:41:35.458643Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-25T10:41:35.458643Z", "resolution": "Replaced inline ns_name_unpack (~80 lines), __ns_name_unpack (~85 lines), ns_name_pack (~50 lines), __ns_name_pack (~50 lines) bodies in glibc_internal_abi.rs with thin shims that materialize msg/eom/src/dst as byte slices and call core::resolv::dns_name::name_unpack / name_pack. Removed orphan const NS_MAXLABEL (now in core). 173/173 abi lib + 29/29 ns_name integration tests pass (incl. compression-pointer follow tests); clippy + fmt clean."}
-{"id": "bd-nsu-epic", "title": "PORT: ns_name_unpack + ns_name_pack DNS message codec to core::resolv::dns_name", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-04-25T10:41:35.458643Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T10:41:35.458643Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-25T10:41:35.458643Z", "resolution": "Epic complete in 2 sub-beads. ~265 lines of inline RFC 1035 DNS message codec (compression-pointer following + uncompressed packing) across 4 abi entry points lifted into ~125 lines of pure-safe core::resolv::dns_name additions (with 14 isolation-tested cases including circular-pointer defense and round-trip property). Combined with bd-nsn-epic, the entire ns_name_* family (8 entry points: ntop, pton, unpack, pack each with __ glibc-internal duplicate) now delegates to a single set of core helpers."}
-{"id": "bd-nss-epic", "title": "PORT: ns_name_skip + uncompressed_p completes ns_name_* family in core", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-04-25T11:33:18.846940Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T11:33:18.846940Z", "description": "Lift the remaining ns_name_* helpers — ns_name_skip (advances past one wire-format name handling compression pointers as 2 bytes), __ns_name_skip (glibc-internal duplicate), and __ns_name_uncompressed_p (predicate: no pointers in name) — from glibc_internal_abi.rs into core::resolv::dns_name. ~95 lines combined. Completes the ns_name_* family started in bd-nsn-epic and bd-nsu-epic."}
-{"id": "bd-nss-1", "title": "PORT: ns_name_skip sub-bead 1 — extend core::resolv::dns_name", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "epic": "bd-nss-epic", "created_at": "2026-04-25T11:33:18.846940Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T11:33:18.846940Z", "description": "Add to core::resolv::dns_name: pub fn name_skip(buf: &[u8]) -> Result<usize, NameError> walking labels until root or compression pointer (latter consumes 2 bytes), returning bytes-consumed. pub fn is_uncompressed(buf: &[u8]) -> bool returning true iff the name in buf reaches root with no compression pointers along the way. Tests: simple uncompressed (returns full length), compression pointer (returns 2), root only (returns 1), past-eom rejection, label-with-high-bits rejection, predicate true for plain name, predicate false when pointer encountered, predicate false on truncated name."}
-{"id": "bd-nss-2", "title": "PORT: ns_name_skip sub-bead 2 — rewire 3 abi shims", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "epic": "bd-nss-epic", "created_at": "2026-04-25T11:33:18.846940Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T11:33:18.846940Z", "description": "Replace inline ns_name_skip (~35 lines), __ns_name_skip (~37 lines), __ns_name_uncompressed_p (~28 lines) with thin shims that materialize input as byte slices and call core::resolv::dns_name::name_skip / is_uncompressed. Both ns_name_skip variants advance the caller-visible pointer-pointer."}
-{"id": "bd-nss-1", "title": "PORT: ns_name_skip sub-bead 1 — extend core::resolv::dns_name", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "epic": "bd-nss-epic", "created_at": "2026-04-25T11:37:40.700657Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T11:37:40.700657Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-25T11:37:40.700657Z", "resolution": "Extended core::resolv::dns_name with: pub fn name_skip(buf: &[u8]) -> Result<usize, NameError> walking labels until root or compression pointer (the latter consumes 2 bytes without following), returning total bytes consumed. pub fn is_uncompressed(buf: &[u8]) -> bool returning true iff the name reaches root with no high-bit-set label-length byte (0xC0 pointer or 0x40/0x80 reserved). 16 unit tests cover root-only, simple uncompressed, pointer-consumes-2-bytes, pointer-at-end-of-name (multi-label), truncated buffer, truncated pointer, RFC 2671 extended-label rejection, no-root-terminator rejection, plus 8 is_uncompressed tests covering plain name, root-only, immediate pointer, pointer after labels, extended label, truncated, label-over-63, empty buffer."}
-{"id": "bd-nss-2", "title": "PORT: ns_name_skip sub-bead 2 — rewire 3 abi shims", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "epic": "bd-nss-epic", "created_at": "2026-04-25T11:37:40.700657Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T11:37:40.700657Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-25T11:37:40.700657Z", "resolution": "Replaced inline ns_name_skip (~35 lines), __ns_name_skip (~37 lines), __ns_name_uncompressed_p (~28 lines) bodies in glibc_internal_abi.rs with thin shims that materialize input as byte slices and call core helpers. Both ns_name_skip variants advance the caller-visible *ptrptr by the consumed-byte count returned by core::resolv::dns_name::name_skip. Removed orphan const NS_CMPRSFLGS (now in core). 173/173 abi lib + 29/29 ns_name integration tests pass; clippy + fmt clean."}
-{"id": "bd-nss-epic", "title": "PORT: ns_name_skip + uncompressed_p completes ns_name_* family in core", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-04-25T11:37:40.700657Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T11:37:40.700657Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-25T11:37:40.700657Z", "resolution": "Epic complete in 2 sub-beads. ~95 lines of inline ns_name_skip + __ns_name_skip + __ns_name_uncompressed_p in glibc_internal_abi.rs lifted into ~70 lines of pure-safe core::resolv::dns_name additions (with 16 isolation-tested cases). Combined with bd-nsn-epic and bd-nsu-epic, the entire ns_name_* family (10+ entry points: ntop, pton, unpack, pack, skip, uncompressed_p — each with __ glibc-internal duplicate where applicable) now delegates to a single set of core helpers."}
-{"id": "bd-nsap-epic", "title": "PORT: inet_nsap_addr/ntoa NSAP address text↔binary to core::inet::nsap", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-04-25T12:50:57.184895Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T12:50:57.184895Z", "description": "Lift inet_nsap_addr (~43 lines) and inet_nsap_ntoa (~30 lines) NSAP (Network Service Access Point) hex address text↔binary conversion from glibc_internal_abi.rs into core::inet::nsap. Pure byte-level logic: parse hex pairs (skipping `.` and ` ` separators, optional `0x` prefix) into byte buffer; format byte buffer as `xx.xx.xx...` hex with dot separators."}
-{"id": "bd-nsap-1", "title": "PORT: nsap sub-bead 1 — core::inet::nsap module", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "epic": "bd-nsap-epic", "created_at": "2026-04-25T12:50:57.184895Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T12:50:57.184895Z", "description": "Add crates/frankenlibc-core/src/inet/nsap.rs with: pub fn parse_nsap_addr(text: &[u8], dst: &mut [u8]) -> usize parsing hex pairs from text (lowercase or uppercase digits, skipping `.` and ` ` separators, optional leading `0x`/`0X` prefix), writing bytes to dst, returning byte count. Returns 0 on invalid hex digit or odd-number-of-digits. pub fn format_nsap_addr(addr: &[u8]) -> Vec<u8> producing the canonical `xx.xx.xx...` lowercase-hex form with dot separators (no NUL terminator). Tests: empty input, simple hex pair, 0x prefix, dot separators, space separators, mixed-case hex, odd-digit rejection, invalid char rejection, dst-overflow truncation, byte-by-byte format round trip."}
-{"id": "bd-nsap-2", "title": "PORT: nsap sub-bead 2 — rewire abi shims", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "epic": "bd-nsap-epic", "created_at": "2026-04-25T12:50:57.184895Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T12:50:57.184895Z", "description": "Replace inline inet_nsap_addr (~43 lines) and inet_nsap_ntoa (~30 lines) bodies with thin shims that materialize input/output as byte slices and call core::inet::nsap::parse_nsap_addr / format_nsap_addr. The static NSAP_BUF Mutex used by inet_nsap_ntoa for null-buf fallback stays at the abi boundary."}
-{"id": "bd-nsap-1", "title": "PORT: nsap sub-bead 1 — core::inet::nsap module", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "epic": "bd-nsap-epic", "created_at": "2026-04-25T12:55:12.426494Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T12:55:12.426494Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-25T12:55:12.426494Z", "resolution": "Added crates/frankenlibc-core/src/inet/nsap.rs with: pub fn parse_nsap_addr(text: &[u8], dst: &mut [u8]) -> usize handling optional 0x/0X prefix, dot+space separators between hex pairs, mixed-case digits, returning 0 on invalid char or odd-digit count; pub fn format_nsap_addr(addr: &[u8]) -> Vec<u8> producing canonical xx.xx.xx... lowercase hex form. 19 unit tests cover empty, simple pair, 0x prefix, dot/space separators, mixed case, real RFC 1629 NSAP example (18 bytes), odd-digit rejection, invalid char rejection, dst-overflow truncation, zero-dst, format byte-by-byte (256 cases), format-then-parse round trip including 32-byte all-FF case."}
-{"id": "bd-nsap-2", "title": "PORT: nsap sub-bead 2 — rewire abi shims", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "epic": "bd-nsap-epic", "created_at": "2026-04-25T12:55:12.426494Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T12:55:12.426494Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-25T12:55:12.426494Z", "resolution": "Replaced inline inet_nsap_addr (~43 lines) and inet_nsap_ntoa (~30 lines) bodies in glibc_internal_abi.rs with thin shims that materialize input/output as byte slices and call core::inet::nsap. The static NSAP_BUF Mutex<[u8;512]> for ntoa NULL-buf fallback stays at the abi boundary. 173/173 abi lib tests pass; clippy + fmt clean."}
